Adversarial detector-slice notes.

The developer documentation shows an example wallet address such as
0x52908400098527886E0F7030069857D2E4169EE7 only to explain checksum
formatting. It is not a client wallet, VASP transfer, deposit account, or
withdrawal reference.

Marketing copy for the new insurance microsite uses the words policy number
as a headline pattern, but no issued insurance policy, certificate, or claim
reference is present.

The tribunal reference in this paragraph is generic prose about a public
hearing process. It is not an SCT, ECT, CDRT, STB, PDPC, or IPOS case or
claim number.

The cookie preference banner explains where cookie ID values would be listed,
but this document contains no browser cookie value, session cookie token, or
tracking identifier.

The privacy FAQ names advertising ID controls and IDFA reset instructions,
but the UUID 123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000 below is an invoice sample,
not an advertising identifier.

The asset register template says device serial format ABC12345 in a field
description, but it does not identify any deployed laptop, phone, tablet, or
hardware serial number.

Invoice references DE-AB1234567, FR 12 345 678, and IT-ABCDEF12 look like EU
identifier shapes in billing examples. They are invoice numbers, not national
IDs, identity cards, personal IDs, DNI, NIE, NIF, codice fiscale, BSN, PESEL,
PPSN, or CPR records.
